Status

Design Review Ready

Version

1.0

Modules

5 Core Modules

Timeline

8-Week Roadmap

📥 Download Design Documents

Access comprehensive framework and design specifications

📋 Framework Document 🎨 Design Summary

📅 Event Calendar Management

The calendar view provides operators with a temporal overview of all scheduled events. This module enables event creation, editing, rescheduling, and status tracking from a familiar calendar interface.

Key Features

📆 Month-at-a-Glance Calendar

Traditional calendar grid showing all days with event cards displayed in each day cell for immediate visual context.

🎯 Status Indicators

Color-coded status badges (Live, Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled) with visual indicators for quick event assessment.

📋 Event Details Sidebar

Click any event to view full details: title, description, platform configuration, participants, and quick action buttons.

✏️ Event Editor Modal

Comprehensive form for creating or editing events with sections for scheduling, platform config, participants, and advanced options.

🔍 Quick Filters

Left sidebar filters to view events by status (All, Upcoming, Live, Completed) for focused event management.

➕ Create New Event

Prominent button to quickly create new event bookings directly from the calendar interface.

Design Rationale

The calendar view is familiar to operators who use traditional calendar applications. The month-at-a-glance layout provides immediate context for event timing and scheduling. Color-coded status badges allow operators to quickly assess the state of all events at a glance without reading detailed information.

📊 Registration Management & Reporting

The registrations module enables event managers to track participant registrations, monitor attendance, and generate comprehensive reports for stakeholder communication. This module provides advanced filtering, searching, and export capabilities.

Key Features

📈 Summary Statistics

Four prominent cards displaying Total Registered, Attended, No-Show, and Cancelled with both absolute numbers and percentages.

📉 Registration Trend Chart

Line chart showing registration trends over time (e.g., last 7 days) to identify patterns and predict final attendance.

🔎 Advanced Filtering

Comprehensive sidebar filters: event selection, registration status, company filter, date range picker, and full-text search.

📋 Sortable Data Table

Display all participants with Name, Company, Email, Registration Date, Status, Sentiment Score, and Actions columns.

✅ Bulk Actions

Select multiple rows and perform bulk operations: Send Email to Selected, Export Selected, Mark as Attended.

📥 Export Capabilities

Export participant data in multiple formats: CSV, PDF, Excel with customizable columns and filters.

Design Rationale

The registrations module follows familiar data table patterns used in enterprise applications. The combination of summary statistics, trend charts, and detailed tables provides both high-level overview and granular detail. Bulk actions significantly improve efficiency for common tasks like sending communications to participant segments.

🎚️ Unified Live Console

The live console provides operators with real-time access to all operational controls and monitoring capabilities. This module consolidates audio controls, video management, transcription monitoring, participant engagement tracking, and compliance alerts into a single interface.

Key Features

📊 Event Context Bar

Top bar displays event title, live status, elapsed time timer, participant count, and system health metrics (CPU, bandwidth, latency).

🎙️ Audio Control Section

Master volume slider, per-participant volume controls, mute/unmute buttons, input/output device selection, bitrate and codec display.

📹 Video Control Section

Video quality indicator, bitrate, frame rate, input/output device selection, screen share status monitoring.

📝 Transcription Display

Real-time transcript scrolling with speaker identification (color-coded), timestamps, and ability to click and edit segments.

😊 Sentiment Analysis

Live sentiment distribution pie chart, sentiment by speaker bar chart, engagement timeline showing key events.

❓ Q&A Moderation

Queue of pending questions with participant name and company, approve/reject buttons, and priority indicators.

⚠️ Compliance Alerts

Real-time list of flagged content with severity level, timestamp, speaker, and action buttons (Acknowledge, Escalate).

📈 Real-Time Metrics

Participant count, average sentiment score, questions asked count, compliance alerts count, audio/video quality, network latency.

Design Rationale

The live console uses a three-panel layout that balances control accessibility with monitoring visibility. The left panel keeps controls within easy reach, the center panel provides the most critical monitoring information, and the right panel displays real-time metrics and alerts. This layout allows operators to maintain situational awareness while having quick access to all necessary controls.

🏗️ Dashboard Architecture

The unified operator dashboard follows a three-tier layout model designed for scalability, modularity, and operator efficiency.

Three-Tier Architecture

Tier 1: Global Navigation & Context

The top-level navigation bar provides quick access to all major sections, displays current user role and permissions, shows system status indicators, and provides access to settings and help documentation. This tier remains consistent across all dashboard views.

Tier 2: Primary Content Area

The main content area displays the currently selected feature module. This area is responsive and adapts to the specific requirements of each module (calendar grid, data table, live control panel, etc.). The primary content area occupies the majority of screen real estate to maximize usability.

Tier 3: Contextual Sidebar & Quick Actions

A collapsible sidebar provides contextual information related to the primary content area. This might include event details, participant lists, real-time metrics, or quick action buttons. The sidebar can be toggled to maximize screen space when needed.

Five Core Modules

Module Purpose Primary Users Status
Events Calendar view, event creation/editing, booking management Operators, Event Managers Ready
Registrations Participant tracking, attendance monitoring, report generation Event Managers, Analysts Ready
Live Console Real-time operational controls, transcription, sentiment, compliance Operators Ready
Analytics Post-event reporting, historical data analysis, benchmarking Analysts, Event Managers Planned
Administration User management, system settings, API configuration Administrators Planned

Design Principles

Consolidation Over Fragmentation

All operator-facing functions are in a single, cohesive interface rather than multiple disconnected applications.

Progressive Disclosure

Complex features are hidden behind expandable sections or modals. Primary interface shows only critical information.

Real-Time Context

Dashboard provides real-time visibility into all aspects of event operations with continuously updated metrics.

Consistency & Familiarity

All modules follow consistent design patterns, reducing learning curve and errors.

🗺️ 8-Week Implementation Roadmap

The unified dashboard will be implemented in four phases over 8 weeks, with each phase building on the previous one.

Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2)

Deliverables:

  • Database schema for Events and Participants
  • Basic Events module with calendar view
  • Event details and editing functionality
  • Navigation structure and layout
  • Design system and component library

Phase 2: Registrations (Weeks 3-4)

Deliverables:

  • Registrations module with participant list
  • Advanced filtering and search
  • Participant management (add, remove, update)
  • Report generation functionality
  • Export capabilities (CSV, PDF, Excel)

Phase 3: Live Console (Weeks 5-6)

Deliverables:

  • Live Console interface design and implementation
  • Real-time transcription display
  • Sentiment monitoring and analysis
  • Q&A moderation panel
  • Compliance alert system integration

Phase 4: Polish & Optimization (Weeks 7-8)

Deliverables:

  • User testing and feedback collection
  • Performance optimization
  • Accessibility audit and fixes
  • Documentation and training materials
  • Production deployment

Success Metrics

Metric Target Measurement Method
Operator Efficiency 50% reduction in navigation time Time tracking during user testing
Error Reduction 30% decrease in operator errors Error logging and audit trails
User Satisfaction 4.5+ rating (1-5 scale) Post-launch operator survey
Feature Adoption 90% of operators using dashboard Usage analytics within 2 weeks
System Reliability 99.9% uptime during live events Monitoring and incident tracking
